priority_categories.name: Emergency Leave
priority_categories.who_qualifies: Active duty, RC members on active duty orders, and their dependents traveling on certified emergency leave (EFMP/humanitarian).
priority_categories.requirements: Commander-verified emergency leave authorization. Travel must be to/from emergency location.
priority_categories.notes: Extremely rare use — authentic family emergencies only. No space-A flexibility; military may also arrange funded emergency travel in crisis cases.

priority_categories.name: Environmental & Morale Leave (EML)
priority_categories.who_qualifies: Active duty members and dependents on Environmental and Morale Leave. Unaccompanied dependents of members serving overseas more than 365 days.
priority_categories.requirements: Valid EML orders. For OCONUS-to-CONUS: member must have been stationed OCONUS for 365+ consecutive days.
priority_categories.notes: Most commonly applies to members stationed at remote/isolated OCONUS locations (e.g., Diego Garcia, Alert, JTFB). The EML Space-A benefit is one of the most valuable quality-of-life perks for these postings.

priority_categories.name: Ordinary Leave (Active Duty)
priority_categories.who_qualifies: Active duty members on approved leave orders.
priority_categories.requirements: Approved leave orders with leave start and end dates. Member must have a legitimate leave period.
priority_categories.notes: The most common category for active duty Space-A travel. Used for vacation travel, visiting family, and R&R. Must have sufficient leave balance.

priority_categories.name: Unaccompanied Dependents (OCONUS)
priority_categories.who_qualifies: Command-sponsored dependents of OCONUS-stationed active duty members traveling unaccompanied.
priority_categories.requirements: Command sponsorship and active duty member must be OCONUS assigned.
priority_categories.notes: Dependents must have a dependent ID card and be command-sponsored. Useful for family members traveling to/from an OCONUS duty station.

priority_categories.name: Permissive TDY / Retirees / 100% P&T Veterans / Activated Reservists
priority_categories.who_qualifies: Members on permissive TDY (house hunting, transition). Retired military of all components. Veterans with 100% P&T disability rating. Selected Reserve and Guard members on activated orders. Retired Reserve members who have reached age 60 and are collecting retirement pay.
priority_categories.requirements: Permissive TDY: valid permissive TDY authorization. Retirees: military retiree ID. 100% P&T: VA disability ID showing P&T status. Activated RC: valid orders.
priority_categories.notes: Most retirees fall into CAT V for CONUS travel and CAT VI for OCONUS. This is the primary category for long-term military retirees. 100% P&T disabled veterans receiving CAT V access is a newer benefit — extremely valuable.
priority_categories.oconus_note: Retirees: CONUS travel is CAT V. OCONUS travel drops retirees to CAT VI.

priority_categories.name: Retirees (OCONUS Travel)
priority_categories.who_qualifies: All retired military traveling on Space-A outside CONUS. Reserve/Guard retirees who have reached age 60. Medal of Honor recipients and their families.
priority_categories.requirements: Military retiree ID. For Reserve/Guard: must have reached retirement age (typically 60) and be drawing retired pay.
priority_categories.notes: The lowest priority for actual seats. OCONUS Space-A travel as a retiree requires maximum schedule flexibility — you may wait days at a terminal. Best strategy: register at multiple terminals, check flight schedules online, and have alternate plans.

how_to_register: In person at the AMC terminal
By email to the terminal (preferred for early registration)
Online via AMC website at amc.af.mil (select terminals)
registration_process.registration_validity: 60 days from date of sign-up. Must re-register before expiration or you lose your date/time stamp priority.
what_to_include_in_email: SSN (last 4 digits) or DoD ID number
Names and ages of all passengers
registration_process.manifesting_process: When a flight is called, eligible passengers with the highest category and earliest registration time are manifested in order. Once manifested, you have a 'return priority' to complete your trip home even if circumstances change.
registration_process.rollcall: Roll call typically 3 hours before departure. Passengers must be present at roll call or they are dropped.
important_rules: Registration is NOT a reservation — you are standby until manifested
Once manifested for a flight, you have priority for a return flight
Registration expires after 60 days — renew before expiration
Passports required for all international travel
CAC or military ID required at all times
Dependents traveling without the sponsor need a command authorization letter
Pets are generally NOT allowed on Space-A flights
Weight limits apply: typically 2 checked bags (70 lbs each) + 1 carry-on
No guarantee of return travel — plan contingency (commercial ticket purchase budget)

terminals.name: Baltimore-Washington International (BWI) / Andrews AFB
terminals.notes: One of the busiest Space-A passenger terminals on the East Coast. High flight volume to EUCOM. Check amc.af.mil for current schedules.

terminals.name: Joint Base Lewis-McChord (JBLM)
terminals.notes: The main gateway for Pacific Space-A travel from the continental U.S. Northwest Coast. High volume to Japan and Korea.

terminals.name: Travis AFB
terminals.notes: Travis AFB is THE primary West Coast Space-A hub for Pacific travel. Extremely busy — arrive early, have flexible dates. Often the first stop before Hickam for WESTPAC destinations.

terminals.name: Ramstein Air Base
terminals.notes: The hub of Air Mobility Command in Europe. Enormous passenger terminal with high flight volume. Essential for EUCOM-to-CONUS and intra-EUCOM Space-A. Integrated with Kaiserslautern military community.

terminals.name: Kadena Air Base
terminals.notes: The major Space-A hub in the Western Pacific. Key node for intra-WESTPAC travel and CONUS connections. Okinawa is an excellent R&R destination in itself.

terminals.name: Yokota Air Base
terminals.installation: Yokota AB (374th Airlift Wing) / USFJ HQ
terminals.notes: The Space-A gateway for Tokyo area travel. Yokota is 35 miles west of central Tokyo. Proximity to New Sanno Hotel makes this a natural first/last stop for Tokyo visits.

terminals.name: Osan Air Base
terminals.installation: Osan AB (51st Fighter Wing) / 7th Air Force
terminals.notes: Primary Space-A point of departure from Korea. Camp Humphreys is nearby — coordinate with USFK travel office. Seoul/Yongsan (Dragon Hill Lodge) is 45 minutes north by highway.

terminals.name: Joint Base Pearl Harbor-Hickam (JBPHH)
terminals.notes: The gateway between CONUS and the Western Pacific. Hawaii itself is a premier Space-A destination. Hale Koa Hotel on Waikiki makes Hawaii one of the most sought-after Space-A trips.

terminals.name: Lajes Field
terminals.installation: Lajes Field, Portuguese Air Base No. 4
terminals.notes: A critical refueling stop on transatlantic routes. Terceira Island itself is a beautiful, undervisited travel destination — volcanic calderas, whale watching, traditional Portuguese culture. Worth a planned overnight.

terminals.name: Naval Air Station Rota
terminals.notes: NAS Rota is the primary U.S. Navy Space-A terminal in Europe. Gateway to Spain, Morocco, and Southern Europe. Rota village and Jerez de la Frontera are excellent local destinations.

terminals.name: Andersen Air Force Base
terminals.notes: Guam is a major Space-A hub in the Western Pacific and a destination in itself. Adjacent to beautiful beaches, diving sites, and CNMI islands. Andersen FamCamp provides excellent accommodation.

terminals.name: McGuire AFB / Joint Base MDL
terminals.notes: Major East Coast Space-A terminal close to New York City. One of the highest-frequency terminals for trans-Atlantic travel.

terminals.name: Dover Air Force Base
terminals.hub_type: Major AMC East Coast Airlift Hub
terminals.notes: One of the busiest AMC airlift wings in the country. High flight frequency to EUCOM destinations. Less well-known than Andrews but often has better seat availability.

terminals.name: NAS Sigonella
terminals.notes: Sicily's NAS Sigonella serves as a Space-A terminal for intra-Mediterranean travel. Access to Italy, Malta, Tunisia, and North Africa. Combine with Sigonella MWR for Sicily tours.

terminals.name: NSA Bahrain (Manama)
terminals.notes: Limited Space-A availability from Bahrain but serves as a key node for CENTCOM-deployed personnel. NSA Bahrain MWR coordinates rotations and leave travel options.

terminals.name: Misawa Air Base
primary_routes: CONUS connections via Yokota or JBLM
terminals.notes: Less-busy terminal than Yokota or Kadena — can mean better Space-A seat availability. Misawa itself is a gateway to northern Japan: Aomori Nebuta Festival, Lake Towada, skiing.

tips_and_strategy.detail: You can be registered at more than one terminal at the same time. If traveling from Travis to Japan, also register at JBLM and Kadena for maximum flexibility.

tips_and_strategy.tip: Use the AMC Travel Site and Facebook groups
tips_and_strategy.detail: The official AMC Travel Site (amc.af.mil) shows upcoming flight schedules. Space-A Facebook groups (SpaceA Travel) post real-time seat availability and tips from travelers.

tips_and_strategy.tip: Always have a commercial airline backup plan
tips_and_strategy.detail: Never rely solely on Space-A for time-critical travel. Build a budget for a commercial fallback ticket — especially for OCONUS travel with a return deadline.
Roll call is typically 3 hours before scheduled departure. If you are not present at roll call, you are dropped regardless of your priority or registration date.
Late fall and early winter (November–February) tend to have better availability than summer for CONUS-to-EUCOM routes, as fewer vacationing families are traveling.
Once manifested on an outbound flight, you receive 'return priority' for getting home. This makes OCONUS Space-A significantly less risky for the return leg.
Standard weight limits are 2 checked bags at 70 lbs each plus 1 carry-on. Military flights are aircraft, not airlines — they can change at any time. Packing light = maximum flexibility.
When registering, you can list multiple destinations. 'Any' as a destination gives you the most flexibility but requires true schedule flexibility.

conus_only: Reserve/Guard not on orders (varies by state programs)
oconus_eligible_with_restrictions: 100% P&T Disabled Veterans (CONUS Space-A CAT V; confirm OCONUS eligibility with specific terminal)
Reserve/Guard retirees at age 60+ drawing retirement pay
not_eligible: Veterans without retiree or P&T status
DoD civilians not on active duty travel orders
Family members without sponsor traveling with them (except command-sponsored OCONUS dependents)

resources.name: AMC Travel Site (Official)
resources.description: Official AMC Space-A information including terminal listings and registration guidance.

resources.name: Space-A Travel Facebook Group
resources.description: Community group with real-time seat availability reports and traveler tips.
